title: dryopteris biaurita
intro: Dryopteris biaurita, also known as the two-auricled wood fern, is a species of fern native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Dryopteris biaurita can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, which are commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
